spec_match.rationale: The demo aligns with the ChromeStatus and spec framing: per-frame encoded audio metadata can drive active-speaker routing, silence suppression, and low-audio redundancy decisions without decoding.

interactive.rationale: Start, synthetic fallback, stop, silence-floor, and active-hold controls update live participant tiles, stats, and diagnostics.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still demonstrates the feature-page promise of using audioLevel metadata for active speaker and suppression decisions.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now exposes unsupported API fallback, microphone permission failure fallback, synthetic no-microphone mode, aggressive/lenient floor warnings, and all-forwarded/all-suppressed boundary states.

references.rationale: The page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/5206106602995712 link and the WebRTC encoded transform spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps local styling on shared CSS variables, pairs button background and foreground colors, and avoids static-card-only output.

openQuestions.title: Run the required chrome-devtools-mcp browser verification
openQuestions.detail: Chrome DevTools MCP is not exposed in this Codex session, so this source-review refresh cannot honestly claim the required browser pass. Before fully closing this critique, open /v139/audio-level-for-rtc-encoded-frames/speaker-dominance/ with chrome-devtools-mcp, exercise Start mic + simulation, Run synthetic fallback, Stop, both sliders, the unsupported or permission-denied fallback if possible, and /v139/audio-level-for-rtc-encoded-frames/conformance/, then record console, network, DOM/readout, and conformance-route evidence.

summary: Source review repaired the broken inline script, added a synthetic fallback path, and surfaced the failure and boundary branches requested by the previous critique. ChromeStatus API and the WebRTC encoded transform spec were checked for feature 5206106602995712; official browser verification remains open because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable in this environment.
